title: Together AI closes $800M at $8.3B, betting the enterprise open-source pivot is here
subtitle: Aramco's Prosperity7 leads the Series C alongside Vista, General Catalyst, and NVIDIA — a signal that inference on open weights is now a real enterprise line item.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Together AI announced an $800 million Series C on July 1 at an $8.3 billion post-money valuation. Aramco Ventures' Prosperity7 program led, with Vista Equity Partners, General Catalyst, Emergence Capital, NVIDIA, March Capital, Pegatron, and SentinelOne's S Ventures participating. Total capital raised now sits at $1.3 billion. Together AI's core business is running open-weight models — DeepSeek, Nemotron, MiniMax, Kimi — for enterprise customers on its own inference infrastructure at a fraction of closed-model API prices. The company said it plans to grow its infrastructure footprint roughly 50-fold over the next five years.

The interesting move is where the money came from. Aramco leading an AI inference round tells you the strategic-investor pool has widened well past the usual venture names, and Middle Eastern capital is now underwriting Western open-source infrastructure directly. Vista's participation signals the same shift on the enterprise-software side: PE money is treating open-weight inference as a durable line of business, not a phase.

Zoom out and the pattern is clearer. OpenAI has raised $122 billion, Anthropic $30 billion, xAI $20 billion — closed-model labs absorb the headline capital, but a parallel infrastructure layer is being funded to run everything else. Together's pitch is that as open weights close the capability gap with closed frontier models, cost-per-token becomes the deciding factor, and the winner is whichever inference stack runs open models the cheapest. This round is a bet that the gap is closing fast enough for that logic to hold.

A takeaway for learners: 'open vs. closed' is not the interesting axis anymore — 'model' vs. 'infrastructure to run models' is. If you're picking where to build a career, the inference layer, quantization, serving, and cost-per-token engineering are quietly becoming as strategic as the model labs themselves. And that layer is far more accessible to work on without $122 billion behind you.

sources.title: Together AI Raises $800 Million at $8.3 Billion Valuation to Make Frontier AI Accessible to All — BusinessWire
